tasks.title: Implement strategy improvement CLI workflow with bucketed stock reports
tasks.description: Add a strategy-improve workflow that samples five years of month-end selection dates, runs strategy selection, evaluates one-month-forward returns, buckets results into four ranges, and writes grouped stock-report artifacts under artifacts/Stratage_improve.
acceptance_criteria: A new CLI command performs 25 sampled selection runs by default using five years x five months per year with deterministic sampling.
Step1-Step3 compute one-month-forward return_pct rows and classify them into the four requested buckets.
Step4 writes grouped stock-report CSV artifacts by selection date and bucket under artifacts/Stratage_improve/<strategy_name>/.
The workflow supports tqdm progress visibility and has automated tests for params and artifact generation.

instruction:
## Task: Strategy improve bucketed stock report (T20)

**Description:** Implement a strategy optimization analysis workflow that samples strategy selections across five years, evaluates one-month-forward returns, buckets returns into four user-defined ranges, and writes grouped stock-report artifacts under artifacts/Stratage_improve.

**Priority:** HIGH

**Acceptance Criteria:**
1. Add a new CLI workflow that defaults to 5 years x 5 random months per year (25 month-end selection dates total) with deterministic sampling.
2. Step0 daily selection exposes --workers default 20 and --missing-history-threshold default 0.2.
3. For every selected stock, compute one-month-forward evaluation price and return percentage.
4. Bucket return_pct into >0.2, (0,0.2], [-0.2,0], and < -0.2.
5. Produce grouped stock-report artifacts by selection date and bucket under artifacts/Stratage_improve/<strategy_name>/.
6. Provide tqdm-based progress visibility and automated tests.

**Your Task:**
Please create a detailed implementation spec for the Coder to implement.

Requirements:
1. Address all acceptance criteria listed above.
2. Define artifact layout, CLI arguments, and bucket boundary rules explicitly.
3. Reuse existing runner/workflow helpers where practical.
4. Resolve ambiguity conservatively and note any non-goals.
5. Keep the spec implementation-ready for coding and QA.

Your response must remain within the Planner role boundary:
- Do not implement code.
- Do not perform QA.
- Do not declare the task complete.

Return valid JSON only.
summary: T20 is a behavior-changing workflow feature and requires a planner-authored spec before coding.
next_action: Wait for Planner to provide the implementation-ready T20 specification.
